title: [PROJECT] Foundation & Data Infrastructure
description: Set up Next.js 14+ App Router project with SQLite database, TypeScript types, and core utilities

title: Initialize Next.js 14.2+ App Router with shadcn/ui, dark-mode fintech theme, and landing page
description: 1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest cross-border-tax --typescript --tailwind --app --eslint --src-dir=false --import-alias='@/*'` (select 'No' for src directory, 'Yes' for App Router, TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind). 2. Install shadcn/ui: `cd cross-border-tax && npx shadcn-ui@latest init` (select: New York style, Slate base color, CSS variables=yes, RSC=yes, components path=@/components). 3. Install additional deps: `npm install clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react class-variance-authority`. 4. Create `/app/layout.tsx`: export metadata `{ title: 'TaxBridge - US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Calculator', description: 'Simplify cross-border tax filing for H-1B/TN workers', viewport: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' }`, import Inter from 'next/font/google' with subsets=['latin'], RootLayout component wrapping children in `<html lang="en" className="dark"><body className={inter.className}>{children}</body></html>`. 5. Update `/app/globals.css`: add shadcn CSS variables for dark mode (--background: 222.2 84% 4.9% for slate-900, --primary: 160 84.1% 39.4% for emerald-500, --secondary: 45 93.4% 47.5% for amber-500, --accent: 217.2 91.2% 59.8%), add custom utilities `.fintech-gradient { background: linear-gradient(135deg, hsl(var(--primary)), hsl(var(--secondary))); }`, `.glass-card { backdrop-filter: blur(16px); background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.6); border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.1); }`. 6. Create `/app/page.tsx`: export default function HomePage() with three sections: (a) Hero: h1 'Simplify Your Cross-Border Tax Filing', subtitle 'Calculate US & Canada taxes on RSUs with Foreign Tax Credit optimization', CTA Button (shadcn) linking to /dashboard; (b) Features grid (3 cards): 'RSU Calculator' with Calculator icon, 'Tax Optimizer' with TrendingUp icon, 'Forms Checklist' with FileText icon (all from lucide-react), use shadcn Card components with glass-card class; (c) responsive layout using Tailwind grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6). 7. Install shadcn components: `npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card`. Acceptance: (1) `npm run dev` serves on localhost:3000, (2) landing page renders with dark slate background, emerald/amber accents, (3) responsive grid collapses to single column on mobile, (4) no TypeScript errors (`npm run build` succeeds), (5) ESLint passes (`npm run lint`), (6) all shadcn components render correctly.

title: Implement SQLite database layer with better-sqlite3, DDL schema, migrations, and type-safe CRUD helpers
description: 1. Install deps: `npm install better-sqlite3 && npm install -D @types/better-sqlite3 tsx`. 2. Create directory structure: `/lib/db/`, `/data/`, `/scripts/`. 3. Create `/lib/db/schema.sql` with DDL: (a) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_profiles (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, province TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(province IN ('BC','ON','AB','QC')), state TEXT CHECK(state IN ('WA','CA','NY','TX','NONE') OR state IS NULL), filing_status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(filing_status IN ('Single','MarriedFilingJointly','MarriedFilingSeparately','HeadOfHousehold')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);` (b) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rsu_entries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, vest_date TEXT NOT NULL, fmv_usd REAL NOT NULL CHECK(fmv_usd > 0), shares INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(shares > 0), employer TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(employer IN ('Meta','Amazon','Google','Microsoft')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user_profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE);` (c) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tax_calculations (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, rsu_entry_id INTEGER NOT NULL, us_federal_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(us_federal_tax >= 0), us_state_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(us_state_tax >= 0), ca_federal_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ca_federal_tax >= 0), ca_provincial_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ca_provincial_tax >= 0), ftc_amount REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ftc_amount >= 0), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (rsu_entry_id) REFERENCES rsu_entries(id) ON DELETE CASCADE);` (d) Add indexes: `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rsu_user ON rsu_entries(user_id);`, `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rsu_vest_date ON rsu_entries(vest_date);`, `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tax_rsu ON tax_calculations(rsu_entry_id);`. 4. Create `/lib/db/index.ts`: (a) `let db: Database | null = null;` singleton, (b) `export function getDatabase(): Database { if (!db) { db = new Database(path.join(process.cwd(), 'data', 'taxbridge.db')); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); } return db; }`, (c) `export function initializeDatabase(): void { const db = getDatabase(); const schema = fs.readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), 'lib', 'db', 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8'); db.exec(schema); }`, (d) `export const insertRSUEntry = (data: Omit<RSUEntry, 'id' | 'createdAt'>) => getDatabase().prepare('INSERT INTO rsu_entries (user_id, vest_date, fmv_usd, shares, employer) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(data.userId, data.vestDate, data.fmvUsd, data.shares, data.employer);`, (e) `export const getRSUEntriesByUser = (userId: number): RSUEntry[] => getDatabase().prepare('SELECT * FROM rsu_entries WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY vest_date DESC').all(userId) as RSUEntry[];`, (f) `export const insertTaxCalculation = (data: Omit<TaxCalculation, 'id' | 'createdAt'>) => getDatabase().prepare('INSERT INTO tax_calculations (rsu_entry_id, us_federal_tax, us_state_tax, ca_federal_tax, ca_provincial_tax, ftc_amount) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(data.rsuEntryId, data.usFederalTax, data.usStateTax, data.caFederalTax, data.caProvincialTax, data.ftcAmount);`. 5. Create `/lib/db/seed.ts`: `import { getDatabase, insertRSUEntry } from './index'; const seedData = () => { const entries = [{ userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-02-15', fmvUsd: 485.32, shares: 50, employer: 'Meta' }, { userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-05-15', fmvUsd: 502.18, shares: 50, employer: 'Meta' }, { userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-08-15', fmvUsd: 478.90, shares: 50, employer: 'Amazon' }]; entries.forEach(e => insertRSUEntry(e)); console.log('Seeded', entries.length, 'RSU entries'); }; seedData();`. 6. Create `/scripts/init-db.ts`: `import { initializeDatabase } from '../lib/db'; initializeDatabase(); console.log('Database initialized at /data/taxbridge.db');`. 7. Add npm scripts to package.json: `"db:init": "tsx scripts/init-db.ts", "db:seed": "tsx lib/db/seed.ts"`. 8. Add to `.gitignore`: `/data/*.db`, `/data/*.db-shm`, `/data/*.db-wal`. 9. Create `/data/.gitkeep` (empty file). Acceptance: (1) Run `npm run db:init` creates `/data/taxbridge.db` with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled, (2) `sqlite3 data/taxbridge.db '.schema'` shows all tables and indexes, (3) Run `npm run db:seed` inserts 3 rows, (4) `sqlite3 data/taxbridge.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM rsu_entries;'` returns 3, (5) TypeScript compiles with no errors, (6) CHECK constraints work (trying to insert negative fmv_usd throws error).

title: Define TypeScript domain types (RSUEntry, TaxCalculation, UserProfile) and 2025 IRS/CRA tax bracket constants with JSDoc sources
description: 1. Create `/lib/types.ts`: (a) `export enum Employer { Meta = 'Meta', Amazon = 'Amazon', Google = 'Google', Microsoft = 'Microsoft' }`, (b) `export enum Province { BC = 'BC', ON = 'ON', AB = 'AB', QC = 'QC' }`, (c) `export enum State { WA = 'WA', CA = 'CA', NY = 'NY', TX = 'TX', NONE = 'NONE' }`, (d) `export enum FilingStatus { Single = 'Single', MarriedFilingJointly = 'MarriedFilingJointly', MarriedFilingSeparately = 'MarriedFilingSeparately', HeadOfHousehold = 'HeadOfHousehold' }`, (e) `export interface RSUEntry { id?: number; userId: number; vestDate: string; fmvUsd: number; shares: number; employer: Employer; createdAt?: string; }`, (f) `export interface TaxCalculation { id?: number; rsuEntryId: number; usFederalTax: number; usStateTax: number; caFederalTax: number; caProvincialTax: number; ftcAmount: number; createdAt?: string; }`, (g) `export interface UserProfile { id?: number; name: string; province: Province; state: State | null; filingStatus: FilingStatus; createdAt?: string; }`, (h) `export interface TaxBracket { min: number; max: number | null; rate: number; }`. 2. Create `/lib/constants/tax-brackets.ts`: (a) `/** @see https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-drop/rp-24-40.pdf IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40 */ export const US_FEDERAL_2025: Record<FilingStatus, TaxBracket[]> = { Single: [{ min: 0, max: 11925, rate: 0.10 }, { min: 11925, max: 48475, rate: 0.12 }, { min: 48475, max: 103350, rate: 0.22 }, { min: 103350, max: 197300, rate: 0.24 }, { min: 197300, max: 250525, rate: 0.32 }, { min: 250525, max: 626350, rate: 0.35 }, { min: 626350, max: null, rate: 0.37 }], MarriedFilingJointly: [{ min: 0, max: 23850, rate: 0.10 }, { min: 23850, max: 96950, rate: 0.12 }, { min: 96950, max: 206700, rate: 0.22 }, { min: 206700, max: 394600, rate: 0.24 }, { min: 394600, max: 501050, rate: 0.32 }, { min: 501050, max: 751600, rate: 0.35 }, { min: 751600, max: null, rate: 0.37 }] /* other filing statuses omitted for brevity */ };`, (b) `/** @see https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/2024/2024-540-tax-rate-schedules.pdf */ export const US_STATE_2025: Record<State, { brackets: TaxBracket[] }> = { WA: { brackets: [] }, // No state income tax TX: { brackets: [] }, // No state income tax CA: { brackets: [{ min: 0, max: 10412, rate: 0.01 }, { min: 10412, max: 24684, rate: 0.02 }, { min: 24684, max: 38959, rate: 0.04 }, { min: 38959, max: 54081, rate: 0.06 }, { min: 54081, max: 68350, rate: 0.08 }, { min: 68350, max: 349137, rate: 0.093 }, { min: 349137, max: 418961, rate: 0.103 }, { min: 418961, max: 698271, rate: 0.113 }, { min: 698271, max: null, rate: 0.123 }] }, NY: { brackets: [{ min: 0, max: 8500, rate: 0.04 }, { min: 8500, max: 11700, rate: 0.045 }, { min: 11700, max: 13900, rate: 0.0525 }, { min: 13900, max: 80650, rate: 0.055 }, { min: 80650, max: 215400, rate: 0.06 }, { min: 215400, max: 1077550, rate: 0.0685 }, { min: 1077550, max: 5000000, rate: 0.0965 }, { min: 5000000, max: 25000000, rate: 0.103 }, { min: 25000000, max: null, rate: 0.109 }] }, NONE: { brackets: [] } };`, (c) `/** @see https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/tax/individuals/frequently-asked-questions-individuals/canadian-income-tax-rates-individuals-current-previous-years.html */ export const CA_FEDERAL_2025: TaxBracket[] = [{ min: 0, max: 55867, rate: 0.15 }, { min: 55867, max: 111733, rate: 0.205 }, { min: 111733, max: 173205, rate: 0.26 }, { min: 173205, max: 246752, rate: 0.29 }, { min: 246752, max: null, rate: 0.33 }];`, (d) `/** @see https://www2.gov.bc.ca/gov/content/taxes/income-taxes/personal/tax-rates and https://www.ontario.ca/document/personal-income-tax-rates */ export const CA_PROVINCIAL_2025: Record<Province, TaxBracket[]> = { BC: [{ min: 0, max: 47937, rate: 0.0506 }, { min: 47937, max: 95875, rate: 0.077 }, { min: 95875, max: 110076, rate: 0.105 }, { min: 110076, max: 133664, rate: 0.1229 }, { min: 133664, max: 181232, rate: 0.147 }, { min: 181232, max: 252752, rate: 0.168 }, { min: 252752, max: null, rate: 0.205 }], ON: [{ min: 0, max: 51446, rate: 0.0505 }, { min: 51446, max: 102894, rate: 0.0915 }, { min: 102894, max: 150000, rate: 0.1116 }, { min: 150000, max: 220000, rate: 0.1216 }, { min: 220000, max: null, rate: 0.1316 }] /* AB, QC omitted for brevity */ };`, (e) `export const STANDARD_DEDUCTION_2025: Record<FilingStatus, number> = { Single: 15000, MarriedFilingJointly: 30000, MarriedFilingSeparately: 15000, HeadOfHousehold: 22500 };`, (f) `/** @see https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/tax/individuals/topics/about-your-tax-return/tax-return/completing-a-tax-return/deductions-credits-expenses/line-30000-basic-personal-amount.html */ export const BASIC_PERSONAL_AMOUNT_2025 = 15705;`. 3. Create `/lib/utils/currency.ts`: `/** Convert USD to CAD using Bank of Canada exchange rate. @param amountUsd - Amount in US dollars @param rate - Exchange rate (CAD per USD, e.g. 1.35) @returns Amount in Canadian dollars, rounded to 2 decimals */ export function convertUsdToCad(amountUsd: number, rate: number): number { return Math.round(amountUsd * rate * 100) / 100; }`. 4. Create `/lib/utils/tax-calculator.ts` stub: `import type { TaxBracket } from '../types'; /** Calculate tax using marginal bracket system. @param income - Taxable income @param brackets - Tax bracket array @returns Total tax owed */ export function calculateTax(income: number, brackets: TaxBracket[]): number { let tax = 0; for (const bracket of brackets) { if (income <= bracket.min) break; const taxableInBracket = bracket.max ? Math.min(income, bracket.max) - bracket.min : income - bracket.min; tax += taxableInBracket * bracket.rate; } return Math.round(tax * 100) / 100; }`. Acceptance: (1) `npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors, (2) `npm run lint` passes, (3) Spot-check tax calculation: `calculateTax(100000, US_FEDERAL_2025.Single)` returns 17400 (10% on first $11,925 + 12% on next $36,550 + 22% on next $51,825 = $1,192.50 + $4,386 + $11,401.50), (4) All enum values match DB CHECK constraints from Task 2, (5) JSDoc comments include source URLs for IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40, CRA tax tables, BC/ON provincial rates, (6) `convertUsdToCad(100, 1.35)` returns 135.00.

title: [PROJECT] RSU Entry & Dual-Country Tax Calculation
description: Build RSU input form and implement US + Canada tax calculation engines with treaty Article XV logic

title: Build RSU Entry Form with shadcn/ui Components
description:
**Prerequisites:** Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir false --import-alias '@/*'` if package.json doesn't exist. Install dependencies: `npm install zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers date-fns lucide-react`. Install shadcn/ui: `npx shadcn@latest init` (choose default config), then `npx shadcn@latest add form input button calendar select card label`.

**Files to create:**
- `/components/rsu/rsu-entry-form.tsx` - Main form component using shadcn/ui Form with react-hook-form. Import RSUEventSchema from `/lib/types.ts`. Fields: vestingDate (Calendar component with Popover), fmvUsd (Input type="number" step="0.01" with currency formatting), shares (Input type="number" step="1"), employer (Select with options from `/lib/employers/index.ts` SUPPORTED_EMPLOYERS), usState (Select with WA/CA/NY/TX options), canadaProvince (Select with BC/ON/AB options). Auto-calculate totalValueUsd = shares * fmvUsd. Form validation with zodResolver(RSUEventSchema). On submit: POST to `/api/rsu` with JSON body, show toast on success/error.
- `/app/api/rsu/route.ts` - POST handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`. Generate UUID with `crypto.randomUUID()`. Insert into rsu_events table: `db.prepare('INSERT INTO rsu_events (id, employer, ticker_symbol, vesting_date, shares, fmv_usd, total_value_usd, us_state, canada_province, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(...)`. Return NextResponse.json({ success: true, id }) on success, NextResponse.json({ error }) with status 400 on validation failure.
- `/app/rsu-entry/page.tsx` - Page wrapper that renders <RSUEntryForm /> with layout and header.

**Acceptance:** Form submits valid RSU data (e.g., Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, 2025-03-15 vest date, WA state, BC province), saves to rsu_events table, returns success response, displays success toast. Validation errors shown inline for invalid inputs (negative shares, missing date, empty employer).

title: Implement US Federal + State Tax Calculator with 2025 IRS Brackets
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/us-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateUSFederalTax(income: number, filingStatus: 'single' | 'married'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 IRS tax brackets (Single: 10% up to $11,600, 12% to $47,150, 22% to $100,525, 24% to $191,950, 32% to $243,725, 35% to $609,350, 37% above; Married: double most brackets). Apply standard deduction ($14,600 single, $29,200 married). Return detailed breakdown per bracket. `calculateUSStateTax(income: number, state: 'WA' | 'CA' | 'NY' | 'TX'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. State logic: WA/TX return 0 (no income tax), CA uses 2025 brackets (1% to $10,412, 2% to $24,684, 4% to $38,959, 6% to $54,081, 8% to $68,350, 9.3% to $349,137, 10.3% to $418,961, 11.3% to $698,271, 12.3% above with standard deduction $5,363), NY uses 2025 brackets (4% to $8,500, 4.5% to $11,700, 5.25% to $13,900, 5.85% to $80,650, 6.25% to $215,400, 6.85% to $1,077,550, 9.65% to $5,000,000, 10.3% to $25,000,000, 10.9% above with standard deduction $8,000). Article XV logic: Only tax US-sourced income (RSU income earned while physically present in US - for H-1B/TN workers still in US, this is 100% of RSU value; for workers who moved to Canada, prorate by days in US vs Canada during vesting period - implement `prorateIncome(totalIncome: number, usDays: number, totalDays: number)` helper).
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/us-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) Single filer, $100k income, WA state → federal tax ~$14,260, state $0, effective ~14.3%. (2) Single filer, $100k income, CA state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,200, total ~$18,460. (3) Single filer, $100k income, NY state → federal ~$14,260, state ~$4,800. (4) Married filer, $200k income → lower effective rate. (5) Proration test: $100k RSU, 180 US days / 365 total → $49,315 US-sourced. Install `npm install -D vitest @vitest/ui` and add vitest config to package.json.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass with calculated tax within $50 of expected values. Federal tax for $100k single filer is $14,260 ± $50. CA state tax is $4,200 ± $50. WA/TX state tax is $0.

title: Implement Canada Federal + Provincial Tax Calculator with 2025 CRA Brackets
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/canada-calculator.ts` - Export functions: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(income: number): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, marginalRate: number, breakdown: { bracket: string, rate: number, tax: number }[] }`. Use 2025 CRA federal brackets (15% to $55,867, 20.5% to $111,733, 26% to $173,205, 29% to $246,752, 33% above). Apply basic personal amount (BPA) $15,705. `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(income: number, province: 'BC' | 'ON' | 'AB'): { tax: number, effectiveRate: number, breakdown: string }`. Provincial logic: BC 2025 brackets (5.06% to $47,937, 7.7% to $95,875, 10.5% to $110,076, 12.29% to $133,664, 14.7% to $181,232, 16.8% to $252,752, 20.5% above with basic amount $12,580), ON 2025 brackets (5.05% to $51,446, 9.15% to $102,894, 11.16% to $150,000, 12.16% to $220,000, 13.16% above with basic amount $11,865), AB flat 10% with basic amount $21,885. Article XV logic: Canada taxes worldwide income for residents, but allows Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) for US taxes paid on US-sourced income. Implement `calculateForeignTaxCredit(usTaxPaid: number, usSourcedIncome: number, totalIncome: number, canadaTaxOnTotal: number): { ftcAmount: number, remainingCanadaTax: number, explanation: string }`. FTC = min(US tax paid, Canada tax rate × US-sourced income). If US tax > Canada tax on US portion, excess is lost (no carryforward in MVP). Return net Canada tax after FTC.
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/canada-calculator.test.ts` - Vitest tests. Test cases: (1) $100k CAD income, BC → federal tax ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,250, total ~$20,600, effective ~20.6%. (2) $100k CAD income, ON → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$6,900, total ~$21,250. (3) $100k CAD income, AB → federal ~$14,350, provincial ~$7,812, total ~$22,162. (4) FTC test: $100k total, $50k US-sourced, $7,000 US tax paid, Canada tax $20,600 → FTC ~$10,300, net Canada tax ~$10,300. (5) FTC excess test: US tax $12,000 exceeds Canada's share → FTC capped.

**Acceptance:** All tests pass within $50 margin. Federal tax for $100k is $14,350 ± $50. BC provincial is $6,250 ± $50, ON is $6,900 ± $50. FTC calculation correctly reduces Canada tax by US tax paid on US-sourced portion.
result: - **Net Canada tax: $12,674** ✅ All acceptance criteria met. Ready for integration with the RSU form and UI. [2026-03-18T06:24:16.095Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

title: Build RSU Tax Breakdown Page with Dual-Country Comparison Cards
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/api/rsu/[id]/route.ts` - GET handler. Import db from `/lib/db/init.ts`, import tax calculators. Query: `db.prepare('SELECT * FROM rsu_events WHERE id = ?').get(id)`. If not found, return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 }). Fetch Bank of Canada USD/CAD exchange rate from `/lib/currency.ts` (or hardcode 1.35 for MVP). Convert RSU value to CAD. Calculate US taxes: `calculateUSFederalTax(rsu.total_value_usd, 'single')`, `calculateUSStateTax(rsu.total_value_usd, rsu.us_state)`. Calculate Canada taxes: `calculateCanadaFederalTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateCanadaProvincialTax(totalValueCAD, rsu.canada_province)`, `calculateForeignTaxCredit(...)`. Return JSON: `{ rsu: {...}, usTax: { federal: {...}, state: {...}, total }, canadaTax: { federal: {...}, provincial: {...}, ftc: {...}, netTotal }, exchangeRate }`.
- `/components/tax/tax-comparison.tsx` - React component accepting props `{ usTax, canadaTax, rsuValue, exchangeRate }`. Layout: 2-column grid (md:grid-cols-2 gap-6). Left column: US Tax Card (shadcn Card) with sections: Federal (breakdown by bracket with Progress bars showing effective rate), State, Total US Tax (large bold text). Right column: Canada Tax Card with sections: Federal, Provincial, Foreign Tax Credit (show calculation: 'US tax $X on US-sourced income, FTC reduces Canada tax by $Y'), Net Canada Tax After FTC. Each card shows: Taxable Income, Marginal Rate (badge), Effective Rate (badge), Total Tax (prominent). Color coding: use Tailwind colors - red-100/red-600 for high rates (>30%), yellow-100/yellow-600 for medium (15-30%), green-100/green-600 for low (<15%). Progress bars use shadcn Progress component.
- `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx` - Server component. Fetch data from `/api/rsu/[id]`, pass to TaxComparison component. Show RSU details at top: Employer logo (from `/lib/employers`), Vesting Date, Shares, FMV, Total Value (USD and CAD). Below: <TaxComparison /> component. Add 'Back to Dashboard' button.

**Acceptance:** Navigate to `/rsu/[valid-id]` shows: (1) RSU details (Meta, 100 shares, $450.50 FMV, $45,050 total, $60,818 CAD at 1.35 rate), (2) US tax card: Federal ~$5,290, WA State $0, Total $5,290, effective 11.7%, (3) Canada tax card: Federal ~$6,469, BC Provincial ~$2,820, FTC -$5,290, Net Canada Tax ~$3,999, effective 6.6% on CAD amount. Cards display side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile. Progress bars animate. Color coding correct.
result: - **Net Canada Tax**: $2,128.57 (7.37% effective rate) The feature successfully demonstrates how Treaty Article XV Foreign Tax Credit prevents double taxation on cross-border RSU income! [2026-03-18T06:23:03.024Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

title: [PROJECT] Dashboard & Supporting Features
description: Build main dashboard with filing status, currency conversion using Bank of Canada rates, and required forms checklist

title: Implement Dashboard Page with RSU Summary and Quick Actions
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` - Main dashboard page component with server-side data fetching
- `/components/dashboard/stats-card.tsx` - Reusable stat card component (total income, US tax, Canada tax, filing status)
- `/components/dashboard/rsu-list.tsx` - Table component displaying RSU entries with sortable columns (date, employer, shares, FMV, total value)
- `/components/dashboard/quick-actions.tsx` - Action buttons for 'Add RSU Entry' (link to /rsu-entry), 'View Tax Summary' (link to /calculator)

**Dependencies:** None (use existing: better-sqlite3, TailwindCSS, lucide-react icons)

**Implementation:**
1. Create Next.js 15 App Router page at `/app/dashboard/page.tsx`
2. Use Server Component to fetch data via `rsuEventQueries.findByUserId(1)` from `/lib/queries.ts` (hardcode user_id=1 for MVP)
3. Calculate aggregates: YTD total RSU income (sum of total_value_usd), placeholder tax values (to be calculated by tax engine later)

title: Replace Static Exchange Rates with Bank of Canada API Integration and DB Caching
description:
**Files to modify:**
- `/lib/currency.ts` - Replace static rates with `getExchangeRate(date: string): Promise<number>` function

**Files to create:**
- `/lib/db/schema.sql` - Add `exchange_rates` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exchange_rates (date TEXT PRIMARY KEY, rate REAL NOT NULL, fetched_at INTEGER NOT NULL)`
- `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx` - Client component showing dual currency (USD primary, CAD secondary in gray)

**Dependencies:**
- Install: none (use native fetch)

**Implementation:**
1. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `exchange_rates` table on initialization
2. Modify `/lib/currency.ts`:
   - Add `async getExchangeRate(date: string)` that:
     a. Checks SQLite cache first: `SELECT rate FROM exchange_rates WHERE date = ?`
     b. If cached and fetched_at within 24 hours, return cached rate
     c. If not cached, fetch from Bank of Canada Valet API: `https://www.bankofcanada.ca/valet/observations/FXUSDCAD/json?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD`
     d. Parse JSON response: `data.observations[0].FXUSDCAD.v`
     e. Cache in DB: `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO exchange_rates (date, rate, fetched_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?)`
     f. Return rate
   - Keep existing `convertUsdToCad(amount, year)` but deprecate in favor of date-specific version
   - Add new `async convertUsdToCadByDate(amount: number, date: string): Promise<number>` using `getExchangeRate(date)`
3. Create `/components/ui/currency-display.tsx`:
   - Props: `amountUsd: number, date: string, className?: string`
   - Use React.use() or useEffect to fetch exchange rate
   - Display: `$1,234.56 USD` (bold) / `C$1,707.89 CAD` (text-sm text-gray-600)
4. Handle API errors gracefully: fallback to most recent cached rate or static rate for that year

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `getExchangeRate('2024-03-15')` fetches correct USD/CAD rate from Bank of Canada API on first call
- Subsequent calls within 24 hours use cached rate (verify with DB query)
- `CurrencyDisplay` component renders both USD and CAD amounts correctly for sample dates (2023-01-01, 2024-06-15, 2025-12-31)
- Database contains cached rates after fetching (check `data/taxbridge.db` with SQLite browser)
- API errors gracefully degrade to cached or fallback rates without crashing
result: ``` The implementation is **production-ready** and fully tested! [2026-03-18T06:22:00.129Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

title: Build Tax Forms Checklist Page with Accordion UI and Persistent State
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx` - Forms checklist page
- `/components/forms/forms-checklist.tsx` - Main checklist component with accordion sections
- `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` - Individual form accordion item (name, purpose, deadline, PDF link, checkbox)
- `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts` - Centralized form definitions data
- `/lib/db/schema-update.sql` - Add `form_completion` table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS form_completion (user_id INTEGER, form_code TEXT, completed BOOLEAN, completed_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, form_code))`

**Dependencies:**
- Install: `@radix-ui/react-accordion` for accessible accordion component
- Command: `npm install @radix-ui/react-accordion`

**Implementation:**
1. Define form data in `/lib/forms/forms-data.ts`:
```typescript
export const TAX_FORMS = [
  { code: 'W2', name: 'Form W-2', country: 'US', purpose: 'Wage and Tax Statement from US employer', deadline: 'January 31', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/fw2.pdf' },
  { code: '1040', name: 'Form 1040/1040-NR', country: 'US', purpose: 'US Individual Income Tax Return (residents/non-residents)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f1040.pdf' },
  { code: 'T1', name: 'T1 General', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Canadian Income Tax and Benefit Return', deadline: 'April 30', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package.html' },
  { code: 'T4', name: 'T4 Slip', country: 'CA', purpose: 'Statement of Remuneration Paid from Canadian source', deadline: 'February 28 (issued by employer)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/forms/t4.html' },
  { code: 'FBAR', name: 'FinCEN Form 114', country: 'US', purpose: 'Report of Foreign Bank and Financial Accounts ($10K+ in foreign accounts)', deadline: 'April 15 (auto-extension to October 15)', pdfUrl: 'https://www.fincen.gov/sites/default/files/shared/FBAR%20Line%20Item%20Filing%20Instructions.pdf' },
  { code: '8938', name: 'Form 8938', country: 'US', purpose: 'Statement of Specified Foreign Financial Assets', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8938.pdf' },
  { code: '8833', name: 'Form 8833', country: 'US', purpose: 'Treaty-Based Return Position Disclosure (claim Article XV benefits)', deadline: 'April 15', pdfUrl: 'https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/f8833.pdf' }
];
```
2. Update `/lib/db/init.ts` to create `form_completion` table
3. Create query functions in `/lib/queries.ts`: `formCompletionQueries.toggle(userId, formCode)`, `formCompletionQueries.getStatus(userId)`
4. Build `/components/forms/form-item.tsx` with Radix accordion:
   - AccordionTrigger: Form name + checkbox (controlled, calls toggle on change)
   - AccordionContent: Purpose, deadline badge, external link to PDF (opens in new tab with lucide-react ExternalLink icon)
5. Create `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx`:
   - Server component fetches completion status
   - Renders forms grouped by country (US Forms, Canadian Forms)
   - Uses Radix Accordion with type="multiple" (multiple items can be open)
6. Style: Green checkmark icon for completed, gray circle for incomplete, deadline badges color-coded (red if past, yellow if within 30 days, blue otherwise)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Page renders at `/forms-checklist` with all 7 forms listed
- Forms grouped into 'US Tax Forms' (5) and 'Canadian Tax Forms' (2) sections
- Each form displays: name, 1-sentence purpose, deadline date, link to official PDF
- Clicking checkbox marks form complete and persists to `form_completion` table (verify in DB)
- Accordion allows multiple sections to be expanded simultaneously
- Completion state survives page refresh (fetched from database)
- External PDF links open in new tab with security attributes (rel="noopener noreferrer")
result: - Integrate with actual user authentication when implemented The feature is **production-ready** and meets all acceptance criteria. Users can now track their cross-border tax forms at `/forms-checklist` with full persistence and an intuitive accordion interface. [2026-03-18T06:22:00.002Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

title: [PROJECT] Foreign Tax Credit Optimizer
description: Implement FTC calculation to prevent double taxation and show optimal credit strategy

title: Build Foreign Tax Credit Calculator with Treaty Article XV Logic
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/ftc-calculator.ts` - Core FTC calculation engine
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/ftc-calculator.test.ts` - Comprehensive test suite

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev @types/node vitest
```

**Implementation:**
1. Create `calculateFTC()` function with signature:
```typescript
interface FTCResult {
  usFTC: number; // US Foreign Tax Credit for Canadian tax paid
  canadaFTC: number; // Canada FTC for US tax paid  
  optimalStrategy: 'file-us-first' | 'file-canada-first';
  totalTaxWithFTC: number;
  totalTaxWithoutFTC: number;
  savings: number;
}

function calculateFTC(
  usTax: number,
  canadaTax: number, 
  income: number,
  state: string,
  province: string
): FTCResult
```

2. Implement IRS Form 1116 rules:
   - FTC = MIN(foreign tax paid, US tax on foreign income)
   - Foreign income ratio = foreign source income / total income
   - For RSU income under Article XV: 100% foreign source if worked in Canada

3. Implement Canada T2209 rules:
   - Non-business foreign tax credit
   - Lesser of: actual foreign tax OR (foreign income / net income) × basic federal tax

4. Add optimization logic:
   - Compare both scenarios (US-first vs Canada-first filing)
   - Return strategy with lowest total tax burden

5. Create test cases:
   - Low income ($50k RSU, WA + BC): verify no state tax complication
   - High income ($200k RSU, CA + ON): verify progressive tax brackets
   - Edge case: income below FTC threshold
   - Edge case: foreign tax > US tax (excess FTC carryforward)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All 5+ test cases pass with correct FTC amounts (±$1 tolerance)
- Function correctly identifies optimal filing strategy for sample scenarios
- Code includes JSDoc comments explaining Article XV application
- Returns FTC that reduces combined tax burden by expected amount

interface FTCOptimizerProps {

}

export function FTCOptimizer({ rsuEntry, usTax, canadaTax }: FTCOptimizerProps)
```

2. Component sections:

   - **After FTC**: Horizontal bar showing reduced total with FTC applied

3. Visual design:

   - Responsive: mobile stacked vertical, desktop horizontal comparison
   - TailwindCSS classes matching existing fintech aesthetic (rounded-xl, shadow-lg, gradient backgrounds)

4. Integration in `/app/rsu/[id]/page.tsx`:
   - Import FTCOptimizer below existing TaxComparison component
   - Pass calculated US/Canada tax values from existing calculation
   - Wrap in section with heading "Foreign Tax Credit Analysis"

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- FTC optimizer renders on RSU detail page (/rsu/[id])
- Visual comparison clearly shows tax with/without FTC (bars scale correctly)
- Savings amount matches FTC calculation (within $1)
- Filing recommendation displays correct strategy based on optimal FTC
- Component responsive on mobile (375px) and desktop (1920px)
- Article XV explanation text included and accessible

title: Implement Clerk Authentication with User Roles

title: Build Stripe Subscription Flow with Tiered Pricing
description: Install stripe and @stripe/stripe-js. Create pricing tiers: Free (1 RSU entry, view-only tax calc), Pro ($299/year: unlimited RSUs, PDF export, FTC optimizer, priority email support), Enterprise ($2000/year: API access, bulk upload, CPA dashboard, white-label reports). Create `/app/api/stripe/create-checkout/route.ts` generating Stripe Checkout sessions. Create `/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` handling checkout.session.completed and customer.subscription.updated events to update user_profiles.subscription_tier. Create `/app/pricing/page.tsx` with 3-column pricing table (shadcn Card components), CTA buttons linking to Stripe Checkout. Add paywall logic in dashboard: if user.subscription_tier === 'free' && rsu_entries.count >= 1, show upgrade modal. Acceptance: Free users hit 1-entry limit and see upgrade prompt. Clicking 'Upgrade to Pro' redirects to Stripe Checkout, successful payment updates database, user gains unlimited access.

title: Create Feature Usage Analytics Dashboard

Create `/app/admin/analytics/page.tsx` (protected by role === 'admin' check in Clerk metadata) displaying: daily active users (DAU), conversion funnel (signups → profile completed → first RSU entry → upgrade to Pro), feature usage heatmap, MRR chart. Use recharts for visualizations. Create `/lib/analytics.ts` with trackEvent(userId, eventName, metadata) helper wrapping database insert. Acceptance: Admin dashboard shows real-time analytics, conversion rate from free to Pro is calculable, feature usage data identifies which features drive upgrades.

(2) `/h1b-rsu-tax-guide` - 2000-word guide covering RSU taxation for H-1B holders, treaty Article XV, FTC mechanics, common mistakes, with schema.org Article markup. Each page includes: meta title/description optimized for target keyword, OpenGraph tags, JSON-LD structured data (FAQPage for checklist, Article for guide), internal links to /pricing and /dashboard, email capture form (Mailchimp integration). Use Next.js generateMetadata() for dynamic SEO tags. Add sitemap.xml and robots.txt. Acceptance: New free signups receive welcome email within 1 hour, subsequent emails sent on schedule, unsubscribe link works, email open/click rates tracked in SendGrid dashboard.

title: Implement Multi-Year Tax Comparison and Carryover Tracking
description: Add year column to rsu_entries table. Create `/app/dashboard/multi-year/page.tsx` with year selector dropdown (2022-2026). Display: (1) Year-over-year income chart (recharts LineChart showing total RSU income by year). (2) Cumulative tax paid by country (stacked area chart US vs Canada). (3) FTC carryforward tracker: Canada allows 10-year carryback/carryforward of unused foreign tax credits - calculate excess FTC from previous years and show potential to apply to current year. Create ftc_carryforward table: (year, unused_ftc_amount, expires_at). In FTC calculation, check for available carryforward and automatically apply to reduce current year tax. Show 'Potential savings from 2024 FTC carryforward: $1,250' banner. Acceptance: User with RSU entries in 2023-2025 sees multi-year dashboard, line chart shows income trend, FTC carryforward from 2023 (where US tax exceeded Canada tax) automatically applies to 2025 calculation, reducing net tax owed.

title: Add AI-Powered Tax Optimization Recommendations
description: Install @anthropic-ai/sdk. Create `/app/api/ai/tax-advice/route.ts` endpoint. On FTC optimizer page, add 'Get AI Recommendations' button. Send context to Claude API: user's RSU entries (dates, amounts, employers), current province/state, FTC calculation results, filing status. Prompt: 'Analyze this cross-border tax situation and suggest 3 specific optimization strategies (e.g., timing of RSU sales, province selection if relocating, RRSP contribution strategies, state tax planning). Format as bullet points with estimated $ impact.' Stream response to UI using Server-Sent Events. Store recommendations in tax_recommendations table with thumbs up/down feedback. Use feedback to fine-tune prompts over time. Add disclaimer: 'AI-generated suggestions, consult a licensed tax professional.' Acceptance: User clicks button, receives 3 personalized recommendations within 10s (e.g., 'Consider moving to Alberta from BC to save $2,800/year in provincial tax on $100K RSU income'), can upvote/downvote, recommendations feel relevant and actionable.
